agenda_pressure: Favor residual-search metatheorems that support finite recognition, decidability, or complexity.
Prefer canonical automata and exact witness interfaces over local booleans or proof refactorings.
Use fragment work mainly when it builds intrinsic residual graphs or candidate trees with later automata value.
Let verified support and counting counterexamples steer work toward handed or residual criteria rather than ordinary-support claims.
Keep separability results only when they are sharp, reusable, and theory-level.
current_bottlenecks: No unconditional subformula theorem for outputs of residualAtomicState.
No control of all states visited on accepting residual runs.
Residual graph acceptance still depends on external graph machinery rather than a self-contained automaton.
Fragment calculi still lack intrinsic residual graphs and candidate-tree interfaces.
The exact criterion for when residual occurrence already implies acceptance is unresolved.
desired_summary_changes: Residual root states become uniformly subformula-bounded by the original sequent.
Accepting residual runs stay inside a finite subformula-bounded reachable region.
Residual recognition is reformulated as a closed internal branching automaton.
One-sided conservativity gains intrinsic residual-graph or candidate-tree witnesses.
Occurrence-versus-acceptance gaps receive sharp residual-state characterizations.
important_verified_counterexamples: Verified counterexample to: Prove a one-sided exactness theorem for ordinary support closure: if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is left-only, then `SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A`; dually, if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is ...
Verified counterexample to: Prove exact completeness of the support closure by showing `∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), SupportClosure Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)`.
Verified counterexample to: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp} {t : String}, (Γ ⇒ A) → occurs_atom t A → ∃ B ∈ Γ, occurs_atom t B
missing_bridges: From residual exactness to subformula-bounded residual roots.
From accepting residual runs to a finite reachable residual region.
From residual graph recognition to a closed branching automaton.
From translation-level one-sided conservativity to intrinsic fragment residual recognizers.
From same-handed counterexamples to exact residual acceptance thresholds.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize residual root and run subformula bounds together with a closed branching residual automaton, taking fragment work mainly when it produces intrinsic residual recognizers or exact same-handed acceptance criteria.
next_direction.label: finite_residual_control
next_direction.rationale: Residual exactness and graph recognition are already proved, while verified failures of ordinary support exactness, same-handed ordinary-support exactness, and counting-style invariants show that summary-level progress now comes from finite residual control and sharp residual-state boundaries.

overexplored_patterns: Singleton or atom-only corollaries already subsumed by arbitrary-context exact theorems.
Booleans, normal forms, or witness refactorings that only package a solved iff.
Ordinary support or counting claims that ignore the handed or residual obstruction.
Fragment statements weaker than intrinsic residual or candidate-tree interfaces.
Local negative sharpening around small examples without a reusable structural criterion.
theory_snapshot: The theory now has exact arbitrary-context derivability through aligned CandidateTree, HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState, and residual-graph interfaces, together with full one-sided conservativity at the sequent and handed-closure levels. The frontier has moved from extensional completeness to finite residual control: the main missing pieces are subformula bounds for residual root and run states, plus a closed internal residual automaton. Verified failures of ordinary SupportClosure exactness, flat atom-occurrence containment, counting bounds, and same-handed ordinary-support exactness force future progress toward residual acceptance criteria and intrinsic fragment witnesses rather than orientation-blind support claims.
